v2:
SUMMARY:
This PR introduces a new @PermissionAllowed annotation-based mechanism to uniformly enforce permission checks on Topic and Connector Controller APIs, resolving inconsistent permission enforcement (issues #2275, #2278). The approach is architecturally sound but has several gaps in endpoint coverage, creates redundant permission checks with existing service-layer validation, and lacks test coverage.

POINTS:
- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/controller/KafkaConnectController.java:~223] `/connector/restart` endpoint (restartConnector method) is MISSING the `@PermissionAllowed` annotation. The service layer already has `MANAGE_CONNECTORS` permission check (KafkaConnectControllerService.java:508-510), but the controller annotation is not applied. This is inconsistent with the PR's stated goal of "uniformly applying permission checks via annotations." Should add: `@PermissionAllowed(permissionAllowed = {PermissionType.MANAGE_CONNECTORS})`.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/controller/KafkaConnectController.java] Duplicate permission checking will occur. Endpoints like `/createConnector` have both the new `@PermissionAllowed(permissionAllowed = {PermissionType.REQUEST_CREATE_CONNECTORS})` annotation AND a service-layer check at KafkaConnectControllerService.java:72-75 (`commonUtilsService.isNotAuthorizedUser(...)`). Same pattern exists for `/deleteConnectorRequests` (service check at line 398) and `/approveConnectorRequests` (service check at line 452). Consider removing the redundant service-layer checks after the annotation-based approach is verified, or document why both layers are intentional defense-in-depth.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/controller/TopicController.java] The diff for TopicController.java changes is truncated/not fully visible. Cannot verify whether `getTopicsOnly`, `getTopicDetailsPerEnv`, `getTopicEvents`, `getAdvancedTopicConfigs`, and `getTopicTeam` endpoints received `@PermissionAllowed` annotations. These endpoints currently have NO service-layer permission checks (TopicControllerService.java has no `checkIsAuthorized()` calls for them) and would be unsecured if not annotated. Author should confirm all 17 controller methods have appropriate `@PermissionAllowed` annotations.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/error/PermissionConstraintException.java] The new exception handler in KlawExceptionHandler.java must be verified to return HTTP 403 (FORBIDDEN) rather than 401 (UNAUTHORIZED). The existing `KlawNotAuthorizedException` handler (KlawExceptionHandler.java:38) returns `HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED`, but permission-denied scenarios (user is authenticated but lacks specific permission) are semantically 403 FORBIDDEN. The new `PermissionConstraintException` handler should return `HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN` to distinguish authentication failures from authorization failures.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/validation/PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor.java] Verify the aspect uses OR semantics for the permission array (user needs ANY ONE of the listed permissions to proceed), consistent with the existing `CommonUtilsService.isNotAuthorizedUser()` implementation (CommonUtilsService.java:166-181) which uses `retainAll()` intersection logic. The existing pattern at KafkaConnectController.java shows `{APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS, APPROVE_CONNECTORS}` meaning either permission grants access.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/validation/PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor.java] Verify the aspect's `@Order` configuration places it after Spring Security authentication completes but before the controller method executes. Without proper ordering, the aspect could fail when `SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication()` returns null or anonymous authentication.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/validation/TopicRequestValidatorImpl.java:62-67] The Bean Validation constraint `TopicRequestValidatorImpl` still performs its own permission checking (`commonUtilsService.isNotAuthorizedUser(...)`) for `REQUEST_CREATE_TOPICS`/`REQUEST_EDIT_TOPICS`. This operates independently from the new `@PermissionAllowed` annotation mechanism. Clarify whether this should be consolidated (remove from validator if `@PermissionAllowed` covers the same endpoints) or kept separate since Bean Validation constraints execute at a different point in the request lifecycle (after controller method invocation vs before via AOP).

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/controller/TopicController.java] Same duplicate permission pattern as KafkaConnectController: `createTopicsCreateRequest` presumably has `@PermissionAllowed` annotation plus `checkIsAuthorized(PermissionType.REQUEST_CREATE_TOPICS)` at TopicControllerService.java:110; `createTopicsUpdateRequest` has check at line 124; `createTopicDeleteRequest` at line 228; `getTopics` at line 1098. Remove redundant service-layer checks to avoid confusion and double-checking overhead.

- [core/src/test/java/io/aiven/klaw/service/TopicControllerServiceTest.java] The PR checklist shows "Tests for the changes have been added" is NOT checked. Critical test coverage is missing for: (1) unauthorized users being rejected by the new `@PermissionAllowed` annotation with proper HTTP status, (2) users with ANY of the OR-listed permissions being granted access, (3) the `/connector/restart` endpoint's missing annotation. Integration tests should verify the full annotation-to-aspect-to-exception-handler pipeline.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/controller/TopicController.java] `getTopicRequestsForApprover` has dual permission check in service layer (TopicControllerService.java:527-528: `APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS`), but the annotation in the diff shows `{APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS, APPROVE_TOPICS}`. Verify this is intentional — approvers should have `APPROVE_TOPICS` (not just `APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS`) to access this endpoint, matching the new uniform permission model.
v3:
SUMMARY:
PR introduces `@PermissionAllowed` annotation with an AOP aspect to uniformly enforce permission checks on Topic and Connector APIs. However, the base codebase lacks the Spring AOP infrastructure required for the aspect to execute, meaning the permission checks will be silently ignored. Additionally, duplicate permission checks remain in the service layer, not all controller endpoints are annotated, and the new exception type lacks a handler.

POINTS:
- [pom.xml] **CRITICAL: Missing `spring-boot-starter-aop` dependency** — The base `core/pom.xml` has no `spring-boot-starter-aop` or `aspectjweaver` dependency. Without this, the `PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor` aspect will NOT be woven and the `@PermissionAllowed` annotations will be silently ignored. Add `<dependency><groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId><artifactId>spring-boot-starter-aop</artifactId></dependency>` to pom.xml.

- [UiapiApplication.java] **CRITICAL: Missing `@EnableAspectJAutoProxy`** — Even with the AOP dependency, explicit proxying may need to be enabled. Add `@EnableAspectJAutoProxy` to the `@SpringBootApplication` class to guarantee aspect execution.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/error/KlawExceptionHandler.java] **Missing handler for `PermissionConstraintException`** — The PR introduces a new exception type but the diff for KlawExceptionHandler shows no `@ExceptionHandler(PermissionConstraintException.class)` method. Without this handler, rejected requests will produce an unhandled 500 error or expose stack traces instead of a clean 403 response. Add an exception handler that returns `HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN`.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/service/KafkaConnectControllerService.java:112,488,508,532,641,791,839] **Duplicate permission checks remain in service layer** — Seven `isNotAuthorizedUser()` calls still exist in `KafkaConnectControllerService`. If the controller-layer `@PermissionAllowed` aspect is meant to replace these, they should be removed. If they're intended as defense-in-depth, document this explicitly. Current state creates redundant authorization checks on every request.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/service/TopicControllerService.java:110,115,124,228,527,682,720,723,885,1098] **Duplicate permission checks remain in TopicControllerService** — Ten `isNotAuthorizedUser()` / `checkIsAuthorized()` calls persist. Same concern as above — either remove them (since the controller aspect should gate access) or justify keeping them as defense-in-depth.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/controller/KafkaConnectController.java] **Not all endpoints annotated** — `getConnectorDetailsPerEnv` and `restartConnector` are missing `@PermissionAllowed` annotations. All public endpoints should be consistently covered. In particular, `restartConnector` currently requires `MANAGE_CONNECTORS` in the service layer but has no controller-level annotation.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/controller/KafkaConnectController.java:88] **Semantic mismatch: `deleteConnectorRequests` uses wrong permission** — The `deleteConnectorRequests` endpoint is annotated with `@PermissionAllowed(permissionAllowed = {PermissionType.REQUEST_CREATE_CONNECTORS})` at line 88, but the service-layer check at `KafkaConnectControllerService:488` also uses `REQUEST_CREATE_CONNECTORS`. Deleting a request semantically should use `REQUEST_DELETE_CONNECTORS` (which already exists as a permission type) rather than a CREATE permission.

- [TopicController.java — diff not provided] **Cannot verify TopicController annotation coverage** — The PR lists TopicController.java as changed but the diff is omitted. Verify all public endpoints receive the appropriate `@PermissionAllowed` annotation, matching the service-layer permission types (`REQUEST_CREATE_TOPICS`, `REQUEST_EDIT_TOPICS`, `REQUEST_DELETE_TOPICS`, `APPROVE_TOPICS`, `APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS`, `VIEW_TOPICS`).

- [Tests] **No tests added for new aspect** — The PR checklist shows "Tests for the changes have been added" is unchecked. The new `PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor` requires unit/integration tests covering: (1) permitted user passes, (2) unauthorized user gets `PermissionConstraintException`, (3) multiple permissions work as OR logic, (4) exception handler returns correct HTTP status.

- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/validation/PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor.java] **Aspect ordering not guaranteed** — Consider adding `@Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRIORITY)` or an explicit `@Order` value to ensure the permission check runs before any other interceptors (e.g., transaction, caching). This prevents unnecessary work when the user is not authorized.
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR introduces an AOP-based `@PermissionAllowed` annotation for declarative permission checking on Topic and Connector APIs. The approach is sound (first AOP-based authorization in the codebase), but there's a critical compilation error, inconsistent permission semantics, and incomplete migration from existing service-level checks.

POINTS:
- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/error/KlawExceptionHandler.java] **COMPILATION ERROR**: The new exception handler references `ApiResponse.UNAUTHORIZED`, which does NOT exist. `ApiResponse` defines only `SUCCESS`, `FAILURE`, and `NOT_AUTHORIZED`. This will fail to compile. Replace with `ApiResponse.NOT_AUTHORIZED` to match the existing `handleKlawNotAuthoirzedExceptionInternal` pattern (which also returns `ApiResponse.NOT_AUTHORIZED` with `HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED`).
- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/controller/KafkaConnectController.java:saveConnectorDocumentation] **WRONG PERMISSION TYPE**: `saveConnectorDocumentation` (POST endpoint that writes documentation) is annotated with `@PermissionAllowed(permissionAllowed = {PermissionType.VIEW_CONNECTORS})`. A write/mutation operation should not use a VIEW permission. Consider adding a dedicated `EDIT_CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION` or `MANAGE_CONNECTORS` permission type, or at minimum use a more appropriate existing permission.
- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/service/KafkaConnectControllerService.java:112,440,452,528,553] **REDUNDANT PERMISSION CHECKS**: After adding `@PermissionAllowed` annotations on controller methods, the equivalent `isNotAuthorizedUser()` checks remain in service methods (e.g., `createConnectorRequest` line 112, `restartConnector` line 440, `deleteConnectorRequests` line 452, `approveConnectorRequests` line 528, `declineConnectorRequests` line 553). This creates redundant double-checking. Either remove the service-level checks or document why both layers are intentionally retained. Also note: the annotation uses OR logic (`{A, B}` = either permission suffices) while service-level checks use single-permission logic, creating potential inconsistency.
- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/controller/KafkaConnectController.java:restartConnector] **MISSING ANNOTATION (diff truncated)**: The diff is truncated before confirming whether `restartConnector` (POST /connector/restart, line 223 in base) receives `@PermissionAllowed`. The service layer (`KafkaConnectControllerService.restartConnector`) checks `PermissionType.MANAGE_CONNECTORS` (line 440). Ensure this endpoint has the annotation: `@PermissionAllowed(permissionAllowed = {PermissionType.MANAGE_CONNECTORS})`.
- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/controller/TopicController.java] **ANNOTATION COVERAGE UNVERIFIED**: The PR description states annotations are added to Topic APIs, but the TopicController diff is not included. The base file shows ZERO `@PermissionAllowed` annotations on all 17 TopicController endpoints. Verify that the TopicController changes are present in the PR and that endpoints like `/getTopics`, `/getTopicRequests`, `/execTopicRequests`, `/execTopicRequestsDecline`, `/deleteTopicRequests`, `/getTopicRequestsForApprover` are covered.
- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/model/requests/TopicCreateRequestModel.java:13] **DUAL PERMISSION PATHS**: TopicController endpoints `/createTopics` and `/updateTopics` already perform permission checks via `@TopicRequestValidator` on the model classes (Bean Validation path). If `@PermissionAllowed` annotations are also added to the same controller methods, requests will be validated twice through different mechanisms. Consider whether the `@TopicRequestValidator` permission check in `TopicRequestValidatorImpl.isValid()` (line 44-48) should be removed if the new annotation handles it, to avoid confusion about which mechanism is authoritative.
- [core/src/main/java/io/aiven/klaw/error/KlawExceptionHandler.java:53] MINOR: Typo in method name `handleKlawNotAuthoirzedExceptionInternal` — "Authoirzed" should be "Authorized". Not introduced by this PR but worth noting.
